evals.name: single-module-java-app
evals.prompt: Create a single-module Kora application structure in Java with the name my-app and package com.example. Include: build.gradle with koraBom configuration, Application.java with @KoraApp, DatabaseModule.java with @Module.
evals.expected_output: Single-module project with correct structure, Gradle setup with koraBom, @KoraApp application, @Module for DI

evals.name: multi-module-architecture
evals.prompt: Create a multi-module architecture for a Kora application with modules: common (shared types), pet-api (Pet domain), vet-api (Vet domain), app (assembly). Show the structure and @KoraSubmodule for each module.
evals.expected_output: Multi-module structure with @KoraSubmodule for each module, proper extends for external modules

evals.name: config-source-interface
evals.prompt: Create a @ConfigSource interface DatabaseConfig with config path 'app.database'. Fields: url (String), username (String), password (String), poolSize (int). Show the corresponding HOCON config with environment variables.
evals.expected_output: @ConfigSource interface with correct path, HOCON config with ${ENV_VAR} syntax

evals.name: component-with-injection
evals.prompt: Create a @Component class UserService with constructor injection. Dependencies: UserRepository, CacheService. The class must be final with a single public constructor.
evals.expected_output: @Component final class with constructor injection, proper dependencies

evals.name: tagged-dependencies
evals.prompt: Show an example of using @Tag to distinguish Cache implementations: RedisCache and CaffeineCache. Create a tag class RedisTag, tagged components, and tagged injection in a service.
evals.expected_output: Simple tag class, @Tag on components, @Tag at injection point

evals.name: root-component
evals.prompt: Create a @Root component CacheWarmer that warms up the cache at application startup. The component must have @Component and a dependency on CacheService.
evals.expected_output: @Root @Component class with initialization in the constructor

evals.name: lifecycle-component
evals.prompt: Create a DatabasePool component that implements the Lifecycle interface with init() and release() methods. The component must be @Component and a final class.
evals.expected_output: @Component class implementing Lifecycle with init/release methods

evals.name: module-with-factory
evals.prompt: Create a @Module interface DatabaseModule with a factory method dataSource() that creates a DataSource from DatabaseConfig. Use @DefaultComponent on the method.
evals.expected_output: @Module interface with @DefaultComponent factory method, parameters from Config

evals.name: all-collection-injection
evals.prompt: Show an example of injecting a collection of all Notifier implementations via All<Notifier>. Create a NotificationService that uses All<Notifier> to send notifications through all channels.
evals.expected_output: Use of All<T> for collection injection, iterating over all implementations

evals.name: lazy-injection-valuedof
evals.prompt: Show an example of lazy injection via ValueOf<Config> to prevent cascading refresh. Create a ConfigService that retrieves Config lazily.
evals.expected_output: Use of ValueOf<T> for lazy injection, retrieval via .get()

evals.name: hocon-yaml-config
evals.prompt: Create configuration for a Kora application in two formats: HOCON (application.conf) and YAML (application.yaml). The config must include: server.port, database.url, database.poolSize, cache.enabled.
evals.expected_output: Both configuration formats with correct syntax, environment variables

evals.name: kotlin-multi-module
evals.prompt: Create a multi-module Kora project in Kotlin with modules: common, api, app. Show settings.gradle.kts, build.gradle.kts for each module, Application.kt with @KoraApp.
evals.expected_output: Kotlin multi-module project with Gradle Kotlin DSL, @KoraApp application

evals.name: lifecycle-wrapper
evals.prompt: Show an example of using LifecycleWrapper.wrap() for a factory method in @Module. Create a CacheModule that creates a CaffeineCache with warmup on init and invalidateAll on release.
evals.expected_output: @Module with a factory method using LifecycleWrapper.wrap()

evals.name: optional-nullable-injection
evals.prompt: Show an example of optional injection via the @Nullable annotation. Create an EmailService that can function without a MailServer (optional dependency).

evals.name: gradle-validation
evals.prompt: Run validation of a build.gradle file for a Kora project. Check: presence of koraBom configuration, annotation-processors dependency, Java 17, application plugin with mainClass.
evals.expected_output: Validation report covering all required elements of build.gradle

evals.name: gradle-wrapper-setup
evals.prompt: Set up the Gradle Wrapper for a project from scratch (Gradle is not installed). Create: gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.properties with version 9.5.1, download gradle-wrapper.jar from the official URL, run initialization via java -cp.
evals.expected_output: gradle-wrapper.properties with correct distributionUrl, gradle-wrapper.jar, initialization command

evals.name: disttar-artifact-build
evals.prompt: Show the command to build the final artifact of a Kora application in tar.gz format. Explain the difference between `./gradlew clean classes` and `./gradlew distTar`, and when to use each.
evals.expected_output: Command ./gradlew distTar for the final artifact, explanation that clean classes is for intermediate compilation checks

evals.name: gradle-troubleshooting
evals.prompt: When running `./gradlew clean` I got the error 'Unable to delete directory... This might happen because a process has files open'. Show how to fix this.
evals.expected_output: Use ./gradlew --stop to stop the Gradle daemon, then retry clean

evals.name: iterative-development-workflow
evals.prompt: Describe the correct workflow for iterative development of a Kora application. Which commands to use and in what order?
evals.expected_output: Minimal code increments → ./gradlew clean classes → ./gradlew test → validate against examples
